text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST
Protocol-Specified On-Trial Restaging Examination

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known Stage IV non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma histology, EGFR L858R-mutant, currently enrolled on Phase II investigator-sponsored clinical trial of savolitinib plus osi for MET-amplified EGFR-mutant NSCLC following progression on third-generation EGFR TKI therapy. Prior history of intracranial metastases treated with stereotactic radiosurgery (Gamma Knife) to three sites (left frontal lobe, right cerebellar hemisphere, left parietal lobe). Currently on savolitinib 300 mg daily plus osi 80 mg daily for approximately 8 weeks. Restaging performed per protocol-specified interval. Comparison made to prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and MRI brain obtained following completion of cycle 4 Paraplatin/pem bridging chemotherapy.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

Technique: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of intravenous iohexol contrast. Axial images were acquired from the thoracic inlet through the pubic symphysis with multiplanar reformations in coronal and sagittal planes. Soft tissue, lung, and bone windows reviewed. Slice thickness 1.25 mm throughout chest, 2.5 mm for abdomen and pelvis.

FINDINGS:

Thorax:

Lungs and Airways: The right upper lobe demonstrates a residual pleural-based mass with spiculated margins measuring 1.4 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 2.1 cm on the pre-trial baseline study obtained after completion of bridging carbo/Alimta). This represents a roughly 33% reduction from the start of trial therapy and a substantial overall reduction from the original diagnostic measurement of 5.8 cm at the time of presentation. The mass maintains its characteristic spiculated, pleural-based morphology with architectural distortion of the surrounding parenchyma, consistent with post-treatment fibrosis admixed with residual viable tumor. There is no new solid, subsolid, or ground-glass nodularity in either lung. The left lung is clear. No endobronchial lesions. No airspace consolidation. No pneumothorax. The central airways are patent.

Mediastinum and Hila: The right paratracheal region (station 4R), previously showing a discrete lymph node measuring 0.7 cm at the time of the prior study, now demonstrates no discretely measurable lymph node -- residual soft tissue in this region is below the threshold for pathologic significance and likely reflects post-treatment fibrotic change. Previously enlarged right hilar lymph nodes are no longer appreciable as distinct nodal masses. No new mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The prevascular, aortopulmonary window, and subcarinal regions are unremarkable. The mediastinal contours are preserved.

Pleura and Pericardium: No pleural effusion bilaterally. No pneumothorax. No pericardial effusion. The pericardium is thin and unremarkable.

Heart and Great Vessels: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. The aorta and main pulmonary arteries are of normal caliber. No filling defects within the pulmonary arteries to suggest pulmonary embolism on this non-dedicated study. Mild atherosclerotic calcification of the aortic arch is noted, unchanged.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: No suspicious chest wall lesion. No axillary lymphadenopathy. Mild bilateral lower extremity subcutaneous edema is noted at the lung bases, consistent with the patient's reported peripheral edema and current diuretic therapy. No rib erosion or cortical destruction.

Abdomen:

Liver: The liver is normal in size with a smooth hepatic contour. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesion, mass, or suspicious hypodensity. No hepatic metastases. No biliary ductal dilatation. The gallbladder is present and unremarkable without cholelithiasis or wall thickening.

Spleen: Normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas: The pancreatic head, body, and tail are well-visualized without focal mass, ductal dilatation, or peripancreatic stranding.

Adrenal Glands: Both adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No adrenal nodule, mass, or suspicious thickening is identified bilaterally. This is notable given the history of NSCLC with previously documented adrenal surveillance. No change from prior study.

Kidneys and Ureters: Both kidneys are normal in size with prompt and symmetric nephrographic enhancement. No renal mass, cyst, or hydronephrosis. No hydroureter. A 3 mm simple cortical cyst is noted in the lower pole of the left kidney, unchanged and of no clinical significance.

Retroperitoneum and Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, para-aortic, or mesenteric lymph nodes. No retroperitoneal mass.

Bowel: Limited assessment in this non-dedicated study. No gross bowel wall thickening or free air. No bowel obstruction.

Pelvis:

No pelvic adenopathy. Uterus and ovaries are not fully characterized on this study but demonstrate no gross abnormality. No free pelvic fluid. Urinary bladder is partially filled and unremarkable.

Bones and Soft Tissues:

No lytic or blastic osseous lesions to suggest skeletal metastases. The visualized vertebral bodies, posterior elements, ribs, sternum, and pelvic bones maintain normal bone density and cortical integrity. Mild multilevel degenerative disc disease in the thoracolumbar spine, unchanged from prior. No pathological fracture.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

Technique: MRI of the brain was performed at 3 Tesla using standard brain protocol including pre- and post-gadolinium T1-weighted sequences (MPRAGE), axial T2, axial and coronal FLAIR, diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, gradient echo (GRE/SWI) sequences, and post-contrast T1-weighted axial, coronal, and sagittal thin-section images. 0.1 mmol/kg gadobutrol administered intravenously. Comparison made to MRI brain performed approximately 9 weeks prior.

FINDINGS:

Site 1 -- Left Frontal Lobe (previously 12 mm, then 7 mm on most recent prior study): A small residual focus of enhancement is identified in the left frontal white matter measuring approximately 4 mm in greatest dimension. There is no surrounding T2/FLAIR signal abnormality to suggest active vasogenic edema. This represents continued reduction in size from the 7 mm measurement on the prior examination and substantial reduction from the presenting measurement of 12 mm. The absence of surrounding edema is a favorable sign. The enhancement pattern is punctate and not rim-enhancing, which is reassuring for continued treatment response rather than early radiation necrosis, though the two may be difficult to distinguish at this size. No restricted diffusion.

Site 2 -- Right Cerebellar Hemisphere (previously 8 mm, then 6 mm on prior study): The right cerebellar hemispheric lesion now measures 4 mm (previously 6 mm). It demonstrates faint nodular enhancement without surrounding edema. No mass effect on the fourth ventricle. The lesion has decreased in size and signal intensity from prior examinations, consistent with ongoing response. No new foci of enhancement in the posterior fossa.

Site 3 -- Left Parietal Lobe (previously 6 mm, then 3 mm on prior study): The previously identified left parietal focus of enhancement is no longer definitively visualized on the current study. No residual enhancement is identified in this region on any post-contrast sequence. This likely represents radiographic complete response at this treated site, though a small residual punctate focus below the resolution of this study cannot be entirely excluded.

Leptomeningeal Assessment: No leptomeningeal or pachymeningeal enhancement identified on careful review of post-contrast sequences, including the sulcal and cisternal spaces, spinal canal dura (visualized craniocervical junction), and cranial nerve sheaths. This is an important and favorable finding given the history of brain metastases.

Calvarium and Skull Base: No calvarial or skull base lesion. No marrow signal abnormality.

Ventricles and CSF Spaces: The ventricular system is normal in caliber and configuration. No hydrocephalus. No midline shift. The basal cisterns are patent. The sulci are appropriate for age.

White Matter: Mild-to-moderate periventricular and subcortical T2/FLAIR hyperintensities persist, consistent with chronic small vessel ischemic disease as noted on prior examinations. No interval increase. No new white matter lesion.

Other: No acute infarct on DWI. No hemorrhage on GRE/SWI sequences. No dural sinus thrombosis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. RIGHT UPPER LOBE RESIDUAL MASS -- CONTINUED DECREASE IN SIZE: The right upper lobe spiculated mass has decreased to approximately 1.4 cm, down from 2.1 cm at the start of trial therapy (savolitinib plus osi). This is consistent with a favorable ongoing partial response to current combination therapy. Cumulative reduction from the original diagnostic size of 5.8 cm is substantial. The residual mass likely reflects a combination of treatment-related fibrosis and possibly residual microscopic or macroscopic viable tumor, a distinction that cannot be made on cross-sectional imaging alone.

2. MEDIASTINAL AND HILAR LYMPH NODES -- RESOLVED: Previously measurable right paratracheal (4R) lymphadenopathy is no longer appreciable. Right hilar nodal disease similarly resolved. No new thoracic nodal disease identified.

3. NO NEW THORACIC, ABDOMINAL, OR PELVIC DISEASE: No new pulmonary nodules, hepatic lesions, adrenal lesions, or skeletal metastases are identified. The abdomen and pelvis remain free of metastatic involvement.

4. INTRACRANIAL METASTASES -- FAVORABLE RESPONSE AT ALL THREE TREATED SITES: All three previously identified intracranial metastatic sites have decreased in size from prior imaging. The left parietal site is no longer visualized, consistent with radiographic complete response at that site. The left frontal and right cerebellar lesions have decreased substantially and show no surrounding edema. No new intracranial lesions identified.

5. NO LEPTOMENINGEAL DISEASE: No leptomeningeal enhancement. This remains an important negative finding.

Overall Assessment: These findings are consistent with a favorable ongoing partial radiographic response to protocol treatment with savolitinib plus osi at the 8-week on-trial restaging time point. There is no evidence of progressive disease in any compartment evaluated.
